mechanism_narrative: Rising monthly retail sales indicate strengthening consumer demand, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show negative mean or Rank IC below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows when RSAFS increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, the cost of capital rises sharply, compressing equity valuations and triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5%, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and a decline in total nonfarm payrolls signals deteriorating labor market health, which reduces consumer spending capacity and forces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when UNRATE increases by more than 0.2pp while PAYEMS declines, with a positive mean 20-day return observed in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a sustained increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 60% of post-shock recovery periods where INDPRO rises, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and prompts expectations of tighter monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns show positive mean or Rank IC above 0.00 when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4%, contradicting the inflation penalty mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly housing starts fall below 1.4 million units, the housing construction sector contracts sharply, dragging down related equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of instances where HOUST drops below 1.4 million, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising federal funds rates and increasing unemployment indicates a policy-induced economic slowdown, which suppresses earnings growth and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to predict negative returns when both series move in the specified direction, with a positive mean 20-day return and Rank IC below 0.02 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: During early-cycle expansion phases, quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% confirms robust economic momentum, boosting equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of early-cycle periods where GDPC1 growth surpasses 2.5%, or the signal shows no statistical significance with p-value above 0.10.
